summary: Iter 42 — Frontend-only verification of v2.7.00 Continue Watching focus-clipping fix. The new CSS rule `.vesper-shelf-section:has([data-focused="true"]) { z-index: 20; }` at /app/frontend/src/index.css L237-239 lifts the active shelf's stacking context above its siblings so the focused CW tile's scale(1.08) overflow + bottom progress bar + 'X LEFT' caption are no longer painted over by the next shelf below. The ContinueWatchingShelf section also now has paddingBottom: clamp(18px, 2vw, 36px) (was 0) giving the scale animation breathing room. All four P0–P3 assertions PASS with the live preview backend, viewport 1920x1080, in WebView-compatible :has() Chrome engine.
critical_code_review_comments: index.css L237-239 — `.vesper-shelf-section:has([data-focused="true"]) { z-index: 20; }` is the correct, minimal fix. Verified at runtime: focusing the CW tile sets the section's computed zIndex to '20' (vs default 'auto'), and focusing a regular poster tile in `networks-shelf` ALSO lifts that section to z-index 20 — confirming the rule applies uniformly to ALL `.vesper-shelf-section` ancestors, not just CW. This is the 'free win' across every shelf row promised in the request. The rule is wrapped as progressive enhancement so older WebViews that don't support :has() still render the row correctly (they just clip slightly on focus, same as before).

ContinueWatchingShelf.jsx L79-92 — paddingTop/paddingBottom now mirror at clamp(18px, 2vw, 36px) / clamp(28px, 3vw, 56px). The previous paddingBottom: 0 was the root cause of bottom-edge clipping in the user's video. Combined with the z-index lift, the focused CW tile (tileBottom=987.78px at default scroll position) now sits comfortably above the next shelf (nextShelfTop=1041.39px) — 53px breathing room.

ContinueWatchingShelf.jsx L335-398 CWTile internal layout — single flex column at `bottom: 14` with: (a) play badge + title in a flex row [items-center gap-3] above (b) 'X LEFT' mono caption beneath, then (c) 4px progress bar flush at bottom: 0. Runtime measurements: caption→progress gap = 10.8px (well above 6px requirement), caption='1h 26m left' visible, progress bar present at height 4px. The title div has whiteSpace:nowrap + textOverflow:ellipsis inline styles so long titles truncate instead of wrapping. Clean, premium layout — matches the user's 'used to be perfect' request.

useSpatialFocus.js L535 `if (e.defaultPrevented) return;` regression check — still working. Single ArrowRight from hero-play-button lands exactly on hero-info-button (verified by reading document.activeElement.dataset.testid before and after the dispatch). No regression from the CSS changes.

Minor observation (not blocking): the TMDB backdrop image at https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/w1280/8GjqRgWGCRPSPDTzBaG7TudjQuU.jpg did not render in the seeded CW tile during my test — only the alt text 'The Bride!' showed in the top-left. This is likely a content-blocking quirk of the headless test session (image domain not warmed / CORS in WebView), NOT a regression of the v2.7.00 fix. The layout, focus ring, caption and progress bar all rendered correctly over the dark fallback background. Real device should be unaffected.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 4/4 priorities pass. P0 focus-no-clip: section.zIndex=20 when focused, tile.bottom (987.78) < nextShelf.top (1041.39) with no overlap, elementFromPoint just inside the tile's bottom edge resolves to a DIV inside the tile (not a sibling shelf). P1 internal layout: caption '1h 26m left' visible, caption→progress gap=10.8px, progress bar 4px flush at bottom. P2 regression on regular shelves: networks-shelf section also lifts to z-index 20 on poster focus (free win confirmed). P3 hero D-pad: ArrowRight from hero-play correctly lands on hero-info (no double-fire).

test_credentials: Seeded onnowtv-profiles-v1=[{id:p1,...}], onnowtv-active-profile-v1=p1, onboarding flags. CRITICAL discovery: the CW localStorage key in the task brief was wrong — task says 'onnowtv-cw-v1' but actual key is `onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:<profileId>` (profile-scoped via /app/frontend/src/lib/profileScope.js). Seeding the wrong key produced an empty shelf. Future testers should write to `onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:p1` (or the legacy unscoped `onnowtv-continue-watching-v1` which the lib auto-promotes on first read).
seed_data_creation: localStorage 'onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:p1' = JSON.stringify([{id:'tt15239678', type:'movie', title:'The Bride!', backdrop:'https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/w1280/8GjqRgWGCRPSPDTzBaG7TudjQuU.jpg', positionMs:1800000, durationMs:6960000, year:'2024', updatedAt:Date.now()}]) — produces a single CW tile at 26%-ish progress with '1h 26m left' caption.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2.7.00 CW focus-clipping fix is rock-solid. The CSS rule + paddingBottom change combine to give the focused tile a clean visual hierarchy above the next shelf below. For future iterations: (1) the correct CW localStorage key is `onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:<profileId>` NOT `onnowtv-cw-v1` — the brief example was wrong. (2) The :has() selector is supported in Chrome 105+ (HK1 WebView confirmed), so the lift works on real device. (3) The same `.vesper-shelf-section:has([data-focused="true"])` rule benefits every shelf row, so any future shelf-clipping reports on other rows should already be resolved by this single CSS rule. (4) Hero D-pad fix from v2.6.99 (useSpatialFocus.js L535 defaultPrevented gate) still works under the new CSS — verified ArrowRight play→info single-step.
rca of the issue: Root cause (pre-fix): Each `.vesper-shelf-section` has `contain: layout style`, which makes it form its OWN stacking context. So even though the focused tile inside has `z-index: 50`, that z-index is scoped to its parent section — siblings sections still paint in DOM order, so the next shelf below paints on top of any overflow from the focused tile's scale(1.08) animation (clipping the progress bar + 'X LEFT' caption on CW tiles, focus ring on regular posters). Fix: lift the WHOLE active section to z-index 20 via `.vesper-shelf-section:has([data-focused="true"])`, AND add paddingBottom: clamp(18px, 2vw, 36px) to ContinueWatchingShelf so the scale animation has actual layout space. The :has() pseudo-class is dynamic, so the lift activates/deactivates the instant focus moves between shelves — no JS needed. Verified end-to-end: focused CW tile's bottom=987.78px, next shelf's top=1041.39px → 53px gap, no clip. Section computed z-index correctly reads '20' while a tile inside is focused, returns to 'auto' when focus leaves.
